Did you use the Portfolio Item Copy app (from your Dashboard OR Reports->Deep Copy Feature) to copy*F43325*for your new Feature? If not, you are missing out on pre-defined, pre-estimated Tasks for Planning, Development, and Testing-- please follow our new Feature Template there! See[
Feature Status Definitions

https://microstrategy.sharepoint.com/sites/OperatingModel/SitePages/Feature-Readiness-Status-Levels.aspx
) for more details! Please delete this text when you've completed the Feature Description.  
1. Description
 (In 1-3 sentences, describe the feature, state the personas involved and how this feature solves their problem. A brief overview of why the feature is necessary, what problem it solves, and the benefits it brings to the user or product)
In Library, after making changes to report in design mode, one must first save the report to return to consumption mode to export the data. The customer is requesting the ability for users to export reports in Library without having to save the changes first, directly from the editor view. This is a crucial step for any ad-hoc analyst who would build a report, not save it and need to export obtained results for further distribution.
2. Use Cases
6. Requirements
6.1 Business / Technical Requirements
(Specific functionalities that the feature must include to accomplish the desired tasks. This may include details about data input, system behavior, and data output.)
i.e.
The dossier should be available to selected MicroStrategy employees.
The prompt and completion tokens should not be available for customers to consume.
This data is only requested for MCE customers since the AI features will only be available for MCE environments.
The system should provide the users with the number of requests, number of completion tokens, number of prompt tokens, execution time, and number of users. These metrics should be available at the Day, Time, DSI, License Type, and Feature.  
•    Due to privacy concerns, we should not store the token sent or returned from the LLM.  
•    Every customer using the AI features will purchase a bundle of a certain number of requests (i.e., 20K requests) they will consume each request based on the feature they are using. If they use the generate dossier for the AI canvas feature that will count as a request, if they ask a question to the AI assistant for the Data whisperer, that will count as a request, etc.  
•    [nice-to-have] Start date and expiration date of the AI license key

) was successfully migrated to Jira.

COMMENT 2025-10-27T21:54:11.129-0400 | Stanislav Gordiienko
@Wei (Irene) Jiang
 
@Qingling Dong
 I propose that we move this feature to Q2 candidate. Would you have any concerns?
---
COMMENT 2025-10-28T14:41:06.782-0400 | Stanislav Gordiienko
@Wei (Irene) Jiang
 
@Qingling Dong
 
We have two more cases (
CGWS-3281
 and 
BCVE2374
) that are not directly, but are related to the topic of report exports to excel (in both WS and Library). We can either track them under the same feature or have two separate ones. I strongly encourage to keep it as one to ensure that scope doesn’t get cut. Below are brief details of these cases.
In WS we don’t have any advanced settings for Excel exports of the report. 
Oddly, we actually have report title option at the application level and Library.
So we should consider these advanced options to be added to WS as well for a consistent experience.
Therefore, proposed scope to this feature is:
-export report in authoring mode (including WS, not just Library)
-export with advanced options such as report title and filters (report and view)
In terms of priority, we should first focus on Report View filters becoming accessible and OOTB feature and then consider these enhancements (potentially mid 2026).
Do you have any concern?
cc: 
@Alejandro Olvera Velasco
 
@Marta Turesko

# HOLDOUT-REGRESSION-002

## Holdout Result

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Feature | BCIN-976 |
| Feature family | report-editor |
| Primary phase | holdout |
| Priority | blocking |
| Regression focus | Promotion/finalization behavior remains stable on another feature |
| Decision | Hold |
| Promote to finalization | No |

## Decision

BCIN-976 should remain in holdout and should not be promoted or finalized from the copied evidence bundle.

## Evidence

- The Jira feature snapshot shows `BCIN-976` is still `In Progress`, with no resolution date, last updated on `2026-03-11`, and a due date of `2026-03-25`.
- The target fix version is `26.04`, and that version is still unreleased with release date `2026-04-17`.
- The feature is customer-backed. The customer-scope export marks `customer_signal_present: true` and lists customer references for Amica Mutual Insurance, Printemps, Stuller, and twinformatics, with Printemps explicitly called out as a requester.
- The feature scope is still moving. A `2025-10-28` comment proposes expanding scope beyond Library edit-mode export to include WS authoring-mode export plus advanced export options such as report title and filters.
- The copied bundle shows no linked issues and no subtasks, so there is no implementation, validation, or release-readiness chain in evidence that would justify promotion/finalization.
- The Jira description still contains template instructions and placeholder requirement text, which is another signal that the feature record is not in a finalized state.

## Holdout Assessment

Stable promotion/finalization behavior on this cross-feature regression case means the holdout checkpoint must keep BCIN-976 gated when the evidence does not support completion. That condition applies here:

- The feature is not resolved.
- The planned release is not released.
- Scope is not demonstrably frozen.
- Completion evidence is absent from the blind bundle.

## Conclusion

This output stays aligned to the `holdout` phase by making only a hold/promotion decision. For BCIN-976, the correct holdout outcome is to block promotion and finalization until the feature has resolved status, stable scope, and explicit completion evidence.

## Execution Notes

- Evidence used: `./benchmark_request.json`, `./inputs/fixtures/BCIN-976-blind-pre-defect-bundle/materials/BCIN-976.issue.raw.json`, `./inputs/fixtures/BCIN-976-blind-pre-defect-bundle/materials/BCIN-976.customer-scope.json`
- Files produced: `./outputs/result.md`, `./outputs/execution_notes.md`
- Constraints honored: worked only in this workspace, used only copied fixtures, no external web access, no skill files read
- Blockers: blind bundle does not include linked implementation/test artifacts or attachment payloads, so finalization readiness cannot be proven from the available evidence
